tagline: Enhance branding with a single entry point for your Databricks account.
what_it_does: The Custom URL feature allows eligible Databricks account admins to claim a branded URL (e.g., `acme.databricks.com`) that serves as a unified entry point for all users and workspaces. This replaces the need for multiple per-workspace URLs, enabling seamless navigation between workspaces without reauthentication. The feature requires accounts to be enabled for unified login and has specific prerequisites regarding domain email addresses and workspace configurations.
why_it_matters: This update improves user experience by simplifying access to Databricks environments, enhancing brand visibility, and potentially increasing user engagement. By providing a single, recognizable URL, organizations can streamline their workflows and reduce confusion among users, ultimately leading to improved productivity and collaboration.
plain_summary: You can now set a custom URL for your Databricks account, making it easier for users to access all workspaces.

capabilities.title: Branded Entry Point
capabilities.desc: Claim a custom URL that serves as a single branded entry point for your Databricks account.

capabilities.title: Seamless Navigation
capabilities.desc: Users can switch between workspaces without needing to log in again after enabling the custom URL.

capabilities.title: Auto-Redirect Option
capabilities.desc: Enable auto-redirect to automatically send users from legacy workspace URLs to the new custom URL.

capabilities.title: Developer Tool Compatibility
capabilities.desc: Support for custom URLs is available in various Databricks developer tools, requiring specific minimum versions.

prerequisites: Account must be a paying, verified customer account.
Admin must have a non-reserved domain email address.
Account must be enabled for unified login.
Account must not use Chrome-managed passkeys.
Workspaces must not use Private Link Inbound.
use_cases: Organizations looking to enhance their brand presence through a unified URL.
Teams needing to simplify access to multiple workspaces for improved collaboration.
Companies wanting to streamline user onboarding and reduce login friction.
limitations: Custom URLs can be changed or revoked by Databricks at any time.
Certain partner connectors do not support custom URLs and must use legacy URLs.

steps.title: Access Account Settings
steps.desc: In the account console, navigate to Settings > Account settings.

steps.title: Claim Custom URL
steps.desc: Click the Claim your URL button to enable the custom URL.

steps.title: Enable Auto-Redirect
steps.desc: Optionally enable the auto-redirect setting for legacy workspace URLs.

steps.title: Access Genie One
steps.desc: Navigate to Genie One at your new custom URL to access assets.

key_takeaway: The new Custom URL feature provides a branded, single entry point for Databricks accounts, enhancing user experience and brand visibility.
